fed_stance.notes: 98.1% probability of no change at March 18-19 FOMC. Current rate 350-375bps.

real_yields_dfii10.notes: Rose from 1.72% (Feb 27) to 1.89% (Mar 12). +17bps = RISING. Data current to Mar 12 (1 business day stale).

gdpnow_q1_2026.notes: 2.7% Q1 2026 estimate. Stable, not accelerating or slowing sharply.

oil_supply_shock.context: Iran war, US/Israel strikes, WTI at $90.77 (+$23.81/wk)

gold_etf_flows.notes: Most recent available data. Gold price pulling back -1.25% today despite safe-haven demand.

hy_oas.notes: Rose from 3.03% (Mar 2) to 3.17% (Mar 12). Data current to Mar 12.

sox.notes: Using 1-month trend (-7.46%) as primary direction signal.

move_index.notes: Using 5-day trend (+22.33%) as primary direction signal. Rate volatility elevated.

eia_crude_inventories.source: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report (week ending Mar 6)

geopolitical_risk.context: US/Israel strikes on Iran, ongoing conflict, WTI surge

yield_curve_2s10s.notes: Oscillating 0.51-0.59 range past week. No clear trend = FLAT.

ecb_stance.notes: ECB expected to hold at 2% through 2026. Next meeting March 19. Hawkish risk due to Iran war inflation.

rate_diff_eur_usd.notes: ECB at 2.0% vs Fed at 3.50-3.75%. Differential favors USD.

eurozone_growth.notes: Eurozone composite PMI 51.9 (3-month high). Manufacturing returned to growth at 44-month high.

rba_stance.context: CBA/Westpac forecast March 17 hike to 4.1%; Bullock said hike possible
rba_stance.notes: RBA hawkish = AUD initially up but risk-off environment dominates.

rate_diff_aud_usd.notes: RBA hiking vs Fed holding = AUD-USD differential widening.

china_pmi.notes: Weight 2x for 6A. Using official PMI (49.0 = contraction) as primary signal. China 2026 GDP target lowest in decades (4.5-5%).

boj_stance.context: Hold this week, hike to 1.0% expected by June 2026
boj_stance.notes: Weight 2x for 6J. BoJ continuing tightening cycle. Japan GDP beat estimates.

rate_diff_jpy_usd.notes: Weight 2x for 6J. BoJ hiking = differential narrowing = JPY strengthening. Bullish 6J.

key_drivers: Rising real yields (DFII10 +17bps to 1.89%) are the dominant bearish driver for equities and metals
Iran war geopolitical risk supports CL (crude oil) and safe-haven JPY (6J)
USD strength (DXY +3.64% 1-month) pressures commodities, FX, and risk assets
BoJ hawkish tightening cycle (hike to 1% by June) supports 6J as safe haven
China PMI contraction (official 49.0) and risk-off environment crush 6A (AUD)

data_quality.notes: FRED data current to Mar 12 (standard 1-day lag). All other data verified from live sources. FOMC meeting March 18-19 creates forward uncertainty - confidence capped at 7/10.
